notes: 728 - 200 Mesh Silica (Sil-Co-Sil 90). 50 lb tier is a bulk discount vs 25 lb (non-monotonic) -- bulk_price/bulk_unit corrected 2026-07-04, see docs/price_list_run_notes.md Run 4.
724 - Gerstley Borate. 50 lb bag price matches the glaze tech's 2026-07-04 quote of $225/bag exactly.

703 - G200EU Potassium Feldspar; "Potassium Feldspar" is IMCO's term vs Glazy's "Potash Feldspar". Largest pack is a 55.14 lb (25 kg) bag, no true 25/50 lb tier -- price_50lb is this 25kg-bag price bucketed to the closest standard column (see conventions.md); bulk_price/bulk_unit use the real 55.14 lb quantity.
25289 - Copper Carbonate. Also sold in 1/4 lb ($8.00) and 1/2 lb ($13.00) minimums below the 1 lb tier tracked here -- sub-1lb tiers aren't tracked in this sheet (see conventions.md).

706 - EPK Edgar Plastic Kaolin; found as "EPK" variant via Kaolin search. ALL tiers currently out of stock, prices are last-listed, not purchasable right now -- affects BOTH Frogskin and "Giggin' for Salvation," flag for the glaze tech. 25 lb tier costs more than 50 lb (non-monotonic).
25479 - Red Iron Oxide (Synthetic) 2563; synthetic variant, natural variant may be available separately. 100 lb tier is the best per-lb rate ($3.75/lb) and the true bulk tier, not 50 lb.
747 - Wollastonite W30; also available as 745 W10 at $2.50-$9.00, different grade.

26802 - Frit 3134; recipe says "Ferro Frit 3134" -- IMCO lists the same material without the "Ferro" manufacturer prefix. 50 lb price matches the glaze tech's 2026-07-04 quote of $135/#50 exactly. This is the flux substituted for Gerstley Borate in "Giggin' for Salvation." Also used as "Ferro Frit 3134" in "Looks Expensive" -- confirmed 2026-07-04 via web search that these are the same Ferro Corporation product across multiple independent ceramic-supply sources; merged the duplicate materials row created during that recipe's import.
710 - Marble Whiting (Calcium Carbonate). 50 lb tier is cheaper than 25 lb, same non-monotonic bulk-discount pattern seen with Silica.

763 - 325 Mesh Bentonite; recipe just says "Bentonite" with no grade specified, chose IMCO's standard/generic grade over the premium "Bentonite 200 Mesh API Grade (Volclay)" ($14-$600) -- flag for the glaze tech to confirm which grade "Giggin' for Salvation" actually uses.

26800 - Frit 3124; recipe ("Looks Expensive") says "Ferro Frit 3124" -- IMCO lists the same material without the "Ferro" manufacturer prefix, same pattern confirmed for Frit 3134 (see conventions.md "Material name variants"). Verified live 2026-07-04: product page confirms "26800 - Frit 3124".
25534 - Lithium Carbonate. Single generic product, no grade variants. Verified live 2026-07-04.
25435 - CMC Gum. Single generic product, no grade variants. Verified live 2026-07-04.

25870 - Tin Oxide. Single generic product, no grade variants. IMCO search for "Tin Oxide" returned a second token-overlap hit (Chrome Oxide Green, sharing the word "oxide") that is not a real match -- ruled out by checking the product page directly. Verified live 2026-07-04.
